currentSprintNote: 3-way parallel sprint SHIPPED 2026-05-04. All 3 masters returned and merged. Sprint 27 (HA + pgbackrest + PG17 ops runbooks): main 130787fe — three deployment runbooks (~2200 lines, no code). Sprint 25 (Tenant lifecycle Pt 3 — scoped export tooling): main 4764a3e0 — TenantExportService + TenantExportRunner + Add_TenantExportRequests migration + admin UI Exports card on TenantDetail.razor + /api/tenant-exports/{id}/download endpoint + audit register note. Sprint 24 (Inspection v1 parity B3 BG services): main f43eb262 — 7 vendor-neutral background workers (ScannerHealthSweep + AseSync + 5 ICUMS pipeline workers; vendor-neutral names with vendor-flavoured option keys for v1-parity); additive IScannerCursorSyncAdapter contract; no migrations. Tests: 447/447 (was 396; +26 from Sprint 24 in Inspection.Web.Tests, +25 from Sprint 25 in Platform.Tests). Pre-pilot at 16/27-41 (39-59%). Sprint 25 migration 20260504205232_Add_TenantExportRequests pending application to live nickerp_platform.

prePilotProgress.note: Pre-pilot scope per plan-file §5 (~/.claude/plans/tingly-launching-quasar.md), reduced 2026-05-04 after §6.1 OCR moved to post-pilot. 16 sprint-equivalents shipped against an estimated 27-41 sprint pre-pilot scope. §6.1 OCR (4-6 sprints) deferred to post-pilot — operational probe found v1's OCR is a broken verification check that doesn't gate pilot. Inspection v1 parity (13-18 sprints) advancing — B1+B2+B3 done (5 of 13-18). Tenant lifecycle COMPLETE (Pt 1+2+3 all shipped). HA + pgbackrest + PG17 runbooks shipped Sprint 27 (deploy-time stand-up remaining is operator work). Pace at 1.5 sprints/week sustained = 7-17 weeks remaining.

_resolvedThisSession.title: v1 Tesseract OCR measures 0% on 1917 prod rows — what now?
_resolvedThisSession.context: Sprint 19 measured v1's Tesseract container-OCR against 1917 fs6000images rows (imagetype='Main') with truth from fs6000scans.containernumber (FS6000 vendor manifest, scan-time, independent of Tesseract). Result: 0/1917 exact-match, 0/2157 check-digit pass. Tesseract operates on full-container LUT JPEGs (no plate-ROI detector in v1). The painted container number is visually unreadable in the LUT-rendered preview. See docs/runbooks/08-ocr-baseline-2026-05-04.md.
_resolvedThisSession.operationalProbe: Code archaeology resolves the operational question without needing user investigation. v1's IImageAnalysisFacade.ExtractContainerNumberAsync(string containerNumber) takes the EXPECTED container number AS INPUT and returns OcrResult.MatchesExpected — it's a VERIFICATION CHECK, not the linkage source. Found at NickScanCentralImagingPortal.API/Controllers/ImageAnalysisController.cs:1787 (analyst-triggered endpoint, not automatic). The actual linkage is fs6000scans.containernumber set at scan time by gate barcode or manual entry. Therefore: 0% baseline means the verification check has been silently failing for years but the system still works because linkage isn't OCR-dependent. Operators never knew the verification was useless.
_resolvedThisSession.resolution: RESOLVED 2026-05-04: §6.1 dropped from pilot scope. v1's container-OCR is a verification check that has been silently broken for years — the system works because linkage isn't OCR-dependent. Florence-2 + plate-ROI moves to post-pilot as a quality enhancement (also unblocks §6.3 manifest×X-ray scoring when picked up). Sprint 19's 1 shipped sprint (eval harness + baseline) stays as a useful diagnostic tool in tree but no longer counts as pre-pilot scope. Pre-pilot estimate shrinks by 3-5 remaining sprints + 1 already-shipped = 4-6 total. Workstream marked deferred-to-post-pilot in prePilotProgress.
options: Drop §6.1 from pilot scope (move to post-pilot). Verification check stays broken in pilot — same posture v1 has had for years. Frees ~3-5 sprints of pre-pilot effort + the GPU-box gating dependency. Recommended given the operational finding.

Keep §6.1 in pilot scope. Restore OCR verification quality + unblock §6.3 manifest×X-ray scoring (which depends on working OCR linkage). GPU box availability remains a gate. Plan §12 phases 3-7 still ~3-5 sprints.
Defer the call until later — keep pre-pilot momentum on other workstreams (Sprint 23+ B3, Tenant Pt 3, etc.); revisit §6.1 priority after a few more sprints land.

resolvedDecisions.title: ITenantContext.SetSystemContext mechanism
resolvedDecisions.resolution: option 2 — explicit IsSystem flag + SetSystemContext() + sentinel '-1' + per-table RLS opt-in clauses + system-context audit register

Sprint 14 dispatches AnalysisService VP6. The 5 staged SQL scripts at tools/migrations/sprint-13-deploy/ are awaiting operator psql application.

Until then, every shipped migration since Sprint 5 is still on disk-only for nickerp_platform + nickerp_nickfinance; nickerp_inspection has the 5 Sprint-12 image-analysis migrations applied + 9 deltas pending.

Scaffolds + label exports + 1-epoch smoke runs landed in Sprint 12; real training is the user's responsibility on a GPU box.

history.name: Inspection v1 parity Batch B3 — BG service specialisation (7 vendor-neutral workers)

items.title: ScannerHealthSweepWorker (periodic vendor-neutral IScannerAdapter.TestAsync sweep across active devices) + AseSyncWorker (cursor-based pull for any IScannerCursorSyncAdapter; in-memory cursor; persistence skeleton) + new additive IScannerCursorSyncAdapter contract in Scanners.Abstractions. WorkerOptionsBase + per-worker options binding Inspection:Workers:<Name>:* config keys with Enabled=false defaults. Telemetry counters + histograms via NickErpActivity.Meter (nickerp.inspection.scanner.health_sweep_*, cursor_sync_*).

Five vendor-neutralised ICUMS pipeline workers — AuthorityDocumentBackfillWorker, AuthorityDocumentInboxWorker (drop-folder; single-tenant for v0; multi-tenant routing deferred), OutboundSubmissionDispatchWorker, OutboundSubmissionResultPollerWorker, AuthorityDocumentMatcherWorker. Each replaces its v1 NSCIM counterpart. Workers are vendor-neutrally named; option section names (Inspection:Workers:IcumsApiPull, IcumsSubmissionDispatch) keep v1-parity continuity for operator config files (architectural decision: vendor names belong in adapters + admin UIs, not in core types).

+26 tests (Sprint24WorkersTests.cs) — coverage across all 7 workers' execution slices, options-bound disabled-by-default behaviour, telemetry emission, three-slot (singleton + hosted-service + IBackgroundServiceProbe) registration. Total Inspection.Web.Tests 219/219 (was 193). Suite total 447/447.

ratifications.title: Worker class names + interfaces are vendor-neutral (per VP3); option section names follow v1's vendor-flavoured shape (Inspection:Workers:IcumsApiPull etc.) for operator-config continuity. Resolves brief tension explicitly.

Cursor state held in-memory in AseSyncWorker; idempotency dedupes via Scan.IdempotencyKey unique index on host restart. No new seen_keys / cursor-state tables — keeps schema surface minimal until multi-host deploy lands.

AseSyncWorker.PersistRecordsAsync ships telemetry + logs but persistence path is deferred until ASE adapter actually exists (it doesn't yet — FU-ase-adapter-plugin). Worker shape is final; persistence settles without changing discovery + cursor + telemetry surface.
B3 added zero EF migrations. Idempotency relies entirely on existing unique indexes on Scan.IdempotencyKey + AuthorityDocument.SourceMessageHash.

deferredFollowups.title: Wire AseSyncWorker.PersistRecordsAsync once ASE adapter exists.
Per-tenant subfolder routing for AuthorityDocumentInboxWorker (today: drop folder is single-tenant; picks first active tenant).
Bounded retry budget + exponential backoff for OutboundSubmissionDispatchWorker transient adapter failures (today: errors stay error until operator requeue).
Optional admin Razor pages for B3 worker tuning (poll intervals, batch limits per environment) — /healthz/workers already shows liveness; dedicated UI is incremental polish.
Promote in-memory cursor map to a small ScannerCursorSyncState entity once multi-host deploy lands.

history.name: Tenant lifecycle Pt 3 — scoped export tooling (TenantExportService + TenantExportRunner + admin UI + download endpoint)

items.title: TenantExportService (RequestExportAsync + GetExportStatusAsync + DownloadExportAsync + RevokeExportAsync + ListExportsAsync) + TenantExportBundleBuilder (3 formats: JsonBundle/CsvFlat/Sql; 4 scopes: All/InspectionOnly/FinanceOnly/IdentityAndAudit) + TenantExportRequest entity + Add_TenantExportRequests migration (20260504205232) — schema fields: Id/TenantId/RequestedAt/RequestedByUserId/Format/Scope/Status (Pending|Running|Completed|Failed|Expired|Revoked)/ArtifactPath/ArtifactSizeBytes/ArtifactSha256(bytea)/ExpiresAt/CompletedAt/FailureReason/DownloadCount/LastDownloadedAt/RevokedAt/RevokedByUserId. Cross-tenant by design; not under RLS. Two indexes (TenantId+RequestedAt DESC; Status+RequestedAt). nscim_app GRANT SELECT, INSERT, UPDATE.

TenantDetail.razor Exports card (Format + Scope dropdowns + Request export + recent-exports table with status badges/sizes/expiry/download-counts; 10s auto-refresh while any row Pending/Running) + /api/tenant-exports/{exportId}/download minimal-API endpoint in apps/portal/Program.cs (pulls nickerp:id claim, calls DownloadExportAsync, streams artifact with X-Export-SHA256 header) + TenantExportRunner BackgroundService (polls every PollInterval default 30s; up to MaxConcurrentExports default 2 per tick; sweeps Completed-and-expired rows on each tick; adopts orphaned Running rows on startup).

+25 tests (16 service + 9 runner) covering request lifecycle / format+scope variants / expiry handling / revocation / admin-only access / concurrent requests / pickup ordering / status transitions / concurrency limit / sweep flips Expired and deletes artifact / failure-mode capture / orphan-adoption on startup. Tests use EF in-memory + tempdir for artifacts; cross-DB read orchestration leaves Postgres-required tests for integration suite once migration is applied. Total 421/421 (was 396).

system-context-audit-register section: Sprint 25 / Tenant-Pt-3 — non-system-context cross-tenant reads. Documents that TenantExportService + TenantExportRunner + TenantExportBundleBuilder do NOT call SetSystemContext() — they mirror Sprint 18 TenantPurgeOrchestrator (raw NpgsqlConnection + per-tenant SET app.tenant_id) so existing per-table RLS USING clauses admit reads of THAT tenant's rows. No new opt-in clause needed. tenancy.tenant_export_requests is intentionally NOT under RLS — same posture as tenancy.tenants (root of tenant graph).

ratifications.title: Brief assumed export service would use SetSystemContext + register-and-opt-in; analogous Sprint 18 TenantPurgeOrchestrator pattern (raw NpgsqlConnection + SET app.tenant_id per-target-tenant) is the correct precedent for cross-tenant per-row reads. Cleaner posture: no new system-context callers, no new opt-in clauses.
tenancy.tenant_export_requests intentionally NOT under RLS — same posture as tenancy.tenants. Cross-tenant admin queries succeed without system-context flip.
Bundle build uses atomic temp-file→move pattern so a runner crash mid-write does NOT leave a half-zip Completed. SHA256 computed over finished file.

Download endpoint gates on Status=Completed && !Revoked && !Expired server-side via DownloadExportAsync. Direct artifact paths are NOT exposed on disk to the client. Every download bumps a counter + emits tenant_export_downloaded audit event.

Defaults: 7-day retention (Tenancy:Export:RetentionDays); concurrency cap 2 (MaxConcurrentExports); poll 30s (PollInterval); filesystem storage at var/tenant-exports/{tenantId}/{exportId}.zip (Tenancy:Export:OutputPath). All configurable.

deferredFollowups.title: Postgres-only integration tests for TenantExportBundleBuilder.BuildAsync (reads via raw NpgsqlConnection; can't unit-test against in-memory provider). Ship alongside live-DB smoke once migration is applied.
S3/blob-store storage option for export artifacts (today filesystem-only).
LISTEN/NOTIFY pickup for TenantExportRunner (today 30s poll). Becomes worthwhile if export volumes grow significantly.
FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED on the runner pickup query — today implementation assumes single host.
Format=Sql round-trip test (best-effort INSERT INTO ... VALUES shape; complex JSON/arrays/custom types may need format-specific work for true re-importability).
Conservative InspectionTablesDefault / NickFinanceTablesDefault lists need maintenance per parity batch as new ITenantOwned tables land.

history.name: HA + pgbackrest + PG17 ops runbooks (deploy-time stand-up surfaced as operator actions)

items.title: docs/runbooks/09-postgres-ha-setup.md (760 lines) — primary configure (wal_level=replica, max_wal_senders, archive_command), pg_hba.conf replication line, nickerp_repl role + physical replication slot, pg_basebackup -R standby init, manual failover §7 (capture LSN, pg_ctl promote, repoint connection strings via NSSM AppEnvironmentExtra), failback §8 (Path A rebuild old primary as standby = canonical; Path B reverse failover; Path C unrecoverable old primary), monitoring (lag > 60s, slot disk-fill, standby-disconnected). Patroni explicitly deferred per ROADMAP §1.

docs/runbooks/10-pgbackrest-backup-restore.md (807 lines) — install (apt/dnf, WSL2 caveat for Windows), v0 stanza (repo1-retention-full=12, archive=14, start-fast=y, delta=y), optional S3/Azure cloud-backed repo with repo1-cipher-pass, cadence (Sunday 02:00 full + 6-hourly incrementals + WAL via cron), PITR §7 (--type=time --target-action=promote), quarterly drill §8 (sandbox host, real restore, sanity queries, drill log), retention 90+30+14, monitoring (backup-lag P1, archive-failure P2, repo-disk P2/P1, weekly pgbackrest verify cron P1).

docs/runbooks/11-postgres-version-lock-pg17.md (625 lines) — lock rationale §2 (MERGE/pgcrypto/pg_stat_io features, LTS to Nov 2029, tooling support, complexity-budget), per-node sweep, pg_upgrade flow (--copy vs --link tradeoff, mandatory --check + backup, --jobs=4, post-upgrade hand-off scripts in order, extension restore, pgbackrest re-stanza), v2 compatibility checklist (pgcrypto only; catalog views unchanged PG14-17; RLS syntax unchanged; Npgsql 9.x compatible), rollback shape (no pg_downgrade — restore-from-backup into fresh older-PG cluster).

Deploy.ps1 +32-line HA-awareness docstring block (script is HA-agnostic, Host= segment lives in NSSM AppEnvironmentExtra, post-failover repointing per runbook 09 §7, all-reads-from-primary lock, sister-runbook pointers). Verified parse OK ([PSParser]::Tokenize); -DryRun produces identical phase plan. No em-dashes (PowerShell 5.1 quirk respected).

docs/runbooks/README.md +35 lines — index extends with rows 09/10/11; planned-change entries (HA stand-up, pgbackrest configure, drill, PG17 upgrade); incident-branch entries (primary down, replication-lag, backup-failed, PITR for data loss); deferred-action list adjusted (drops 'Multi-host failover'; adds 'Auto-failover Patroni' + 'Cross-region DR' matching ROADMAP §1 answer 3); footnote on slots 07+08 (analytical, not symptom-driven).

ratifications.title: Sprint produced documentation only — no .cs / .razor / .csproj / migration changes. Test floor 396/396 preserved by construction. Brief explicitly drew the line at runbook-side; HA stand-up itself is deploy-time operator work, not Sprint scope.

Deploy.ps1 docstring touch-up was permitted only because edit was small + additive (no behaviour change). Parse + DryRun verified post-edit. Brief had this as 'optional'; landed because the docstring is the on-disk explanation operators read first.

README index updated, not rewritten — kept slots 07 (incident-only Sprint 13 deploy) and 08 (analytical OCR baseline) intact with footnote explaining their non-decision-tree posture. Decision tree extended for symptomatic scenarios only.

Master surfaced 7 deploy-time operator actions (provision standby box / pgbackrest install / repo location choice / cadence wiring / quarterly drill / alert wiring / pre-pg_upgrade source-version sweep). Tracker operatorActions[] gained 4 of these (provision-standby-box, install-pgbackrest, wire-backup-cadence, wire-ha-alerts) — the others are sub-tasks within these.

deferredFollowups.title: Patroni / pg_auto_failover evaluation post-pilot if operator team grows past one person (auto-failover MTTR vs split-brain trade-off shifts).
Cross-region DR — single-region locked v0; full DR with hot standby in second region post-pilot. Runbook 09+10 primitives compose; missing piece is separate region's repo + standby box.
Runbook 07 §4.5 backup step references pg_dump -Fc; once pgbackrest is in production, swap to pgbackrest --type=full backup. Trivial doc edit, not done in Sprint 27 to avoid touching unrelated prose.
Runbook 10 §5.1 documents WSL2 path as v0-acceptable but flags it as best-effort. Long-term cleaner shape: Postgres on Windows + pgbackrest on separate Linux backup host via SSH.
audit.events partitioning — runbook 11 §2.1 mentions PG14+ partition pruning improvements as latent value; actual partitioning migration is on deferred-action list and benefits from PG17 lock.
NickFinance runbook (G2 has shipped Sprint 10) — README 'what's not yet in this tree' lists module-specific runbooks as out-of-scope; finance-specific runbook may be worth adding next sprint.

history.name: OCR eval harness offline + v1 Tesseract baseline (plan §12 Phase 1+2)

items.title: tools/inference-evaluation/container-ocr scaffold (NickERP.Tools.OcrEvaluation.csproj — .NET 10 console app, IContainerNumberRecognizer reference, Microsoft.Extensions.Hosting DI/logging/config).

Tesseract engine (5.2.0 — same NuGet version v1 ships, copying v1's exact preprocessing chain Otsu+Gaussian+CLAHE byte-for-byte) + Postgres corpus source (streams fs6000images JOIN fs6000scans, read-only) + JSON output schema per plan §12.3.
Directory corpus source (.png/.jpg/.tiff + .json sidecar pairs) + failure-mode classifier (stylized typography / weathering / oblique / false-positive surfaces).

Live-probe schema fixes — truth source amended from non-existent containerannotations.type='ocr_correction' to fs6000scans.containernumber (FS6000 vendor manifest, scan-time, non-circular vs Tesseract).

v1 Tesseract baseline run on prod corpus (2157 rows imagetype='Main', 1917 scored after ISO 6346 mod-11 truth filter, 240 truth-invalid). RESULT: 0/1917 exact-match (0.0%), 0/2157 check-digit pass (0.0%). Latency p50/p95/p99 = 666/1306/1750 ms (CPU). Wall-clock 1562s / ~26 min. Result JSON committed.

docs/runbooks/08-ocr-baseline-2026-05-04.md — narrative report. Decision-gate read: Florence-2 ROI unbounded; §6.1 pilot scope strongly justified. Caveats: input shape (full-container LUT JPEG, no plate-ROI), truth source (FS6000 manifest), no analyst-corrected gold labels available on this prod DB.

ratifications.title: Truth source = fs6000scans.containernumber (FS6000 vendor manifest field, set at scan time by gate barcode or manual entry, independent of any Tesseract pass). Non-circular. The harvester at tools/inference-training/container-ocr/harvest_plates.py assumes containerannotations.type='ocr_correction' rows that do NOT exist on this prod DB (74 rows total, all bbox-only Rectangle type).

Filter to imagetype='Main' only. Matches v1's ContainerNumberOcrService production behaviour (FS6000ImagePipeline.GetCompleteContainerDataAsync feeds only Main). HE/LE/Material would inflate miss rate without measuring v1's reality.

Inputs are full-container LUT JPEGs, NOT plate ROIs. v1 has no plate-ROI detector. Florence-2 in §6.1 will be fed cropped 384x384 ROIs — comparison will be Florence-2+ROI-detector vs v1 status quo, not Tesseract-vs-Florence-2 head-to-head.

EM=0% is a measured reality, not a harness bug. Probe images dumped (probe-00-MSNU1427384.jpg etc.) confirm full-container X-ray scans where the painted container number is visually unreadable in the LUT-rendered preview — the §6.1.1 'mistaken-for-text container surfaces' failure mode the spec calls out. Tesseract genuinely cannot produce ANY valid 11-char ISO 6346 candidate.

history.name: Inspection v1 parity Batch B2 — ICUMS submission/download queues + dashboard + loose cargo + BOE lookup + manual pull

items.title: ICUMS submission queue UI at /admin/icums/submission-queue + IcumsSubmissionQueueAdminService (paged list with status/system/date/search filters; single + bulk requeue bounded at 1000 rows; payload fetch). OutboundSubmission gains Priority + LastAttemptAt + composite index ix_outbound_tenant_status_priority_time. JsonPayloadViewer reusable component lands in Components/Shared/.

ICUMS download queue UI at /admin/icums/download-queue + IcumsDownloadQueueAdminService (reads AuthorityDocument; doc-type/source/match-status filters; admin re-link override). OutcomePullCursor table renders with lag-time chips (≤1h/≤6h/≤24h/>24h thresholds).

ICUMS dashboard at /admin/icums + sub-pages: loose-cargo (cases > 4h with zero AuthorityDocuments), boe-lookup (search by reference/container/date), manual-pull (admin-triggered IInboundOutcomeAdapter.FetchOutcomesAsync via IPluginRegistry; routes through PostHocOutcomeWriter; cursor not advanced).
+25 unit tests across submission/download admin services + dashboard suite + manual pull (193 in NickERP.Inspection.Web.Tests).

ratifications.title: ICUMS appears in /admin/icums/... URLs + UI text only (operationally meaningful to operators); underlying queries hit vendor-neutral entities (OutboundSubmission, AuthorityDocument, OutcomePullCursor).
MaxBulkRequeueRows = 1000; bulk requeue requires explicit status filter; emits single inspection.icums.submission_bulk_requeued audit event per batch (not per-row).
Manual pull does NOT advance OutcomePullCursor — manual pulls coexist with the periodic OutcomePullWorker; idempotency dedup makes overlapping windows harmless.

EF in-memory provider drops rows when FK is Guid.Empty in Select projections (effectively INNER JOIN). Two-step projection (scalars + in-memory dictionary lookup) added to IcumsDownloadQueueAdminService.ListAsync + GetPullCursorsAsync + IcumsBoeLookupService.SearchAsync. Postgres tolerates the original LEFT JOIN; rewrite keeps test parity clean. Followup: Testcontainers-Postgres tests would unlock the cleaner LEFT JOIN.

history.name: Tenant lifecycle Pt 2 — email service + invite tokens + first-user invite flow

items.title: IEmailSender abstraction + FileSystemEmailSender (dev default; .eml to var/email-outbox/) + SmtpEmailSender (MailKit; TLS-required) + NoOpEmailSender (prod default until configured) + AddNickErpEmail DI helper.

InviteToken entity + Add_InviteTokens migration (RLS + force + tenant_isolation_invite_tokens with -1 opt-in; unique partial index on TokenHash WHERE active for race-safe single-use) + InviteService (Issue/Redeem/MarkRedeemed/Revoke/List with HMAC token hashing).

Tenants.razor invite section + AcceptInvite.razor at /invite/accept/{token} ([AllowAnonymous] + antiforgery; CF Access handoff post-redemption since v2 has no local password) + portal email wiring + invite email templates (embedded resources, manifest dash->underscore).
+27 tests across EmailSenderTests + InviteServiceTests + InviteTokenHasher (81 in NickERP.Platform.Tests). Total 371/371.
system-context-audit-register entries: InviteService.RedeemInviteAsync + InviteService.MarkRedeemedAsync + AcceptInvite.razor lifecycle. New table opt-in: identity.invite_tokens.

MailKit 4.9.0 -> 4.16.0 (NU1902 CVE GHSA-9j88-vvj5-vhgr); null-coalesce TextPart.Text for 4.16's non-nullable setter; sub-agent's local NuGet cache forced 4.9 pin, top-level shell has full network so resolved by version bump.

ratifications.title: AcceptInvite does NOT collect a password. NickERP identity is CF Access JWT-driven; no PasswordHash column exists. Page provisions IdentityUser + UserScope + hands off to CF Access portal. Differs from brief which assumed local password.
AddNickErpEmail defaults to NoOp outside Development (stricter than brief's filesystem default) — fresh prod boxes silently log invite emails until operator opts in to a real provider.
MailKit pinned 4.16.0 (latest stable), bumped from 4.9.0 to clear NU1902 CVE. Sub-agent's environment had only 4.9 cached; orchestrator-level shell resolved with full network access.

history.name: Inspection v1 parity Batch B1 — case detail tabs + analyst image viewer + multi-scan nav

items.title: Case detail tabs (Overview / ICUMS / Scanner data / Image gallery / Rules + findings / History) — query-string-bound [SupplyParameterFromQuery] Tab; lazy-load per tab on activation; workflow actions reset all caches.

Analyst image viewer with annotations (AnalystAnnotationService + /api/inspection/annotations + Inspect/Annotate tool toggle + per-severity overlay; findings persist as FindingType=analyst.annotation on find-or-create AnalystReview) + multi-scan navigation (prev/next pager + predictive prefetch via Fetch priority hints).
+8 unit tests (5 annotation service + 3 case detail tabs); InMemoryAuditDbContext + JsonDocument↔string converter helper for jsonb-typed Payload tests.

ratifications.title: Tabs use query-string-bound [SupplyParameterFromQuery] Tab on the existing /cases/{id} route rather than separate Razor pages — keeps URL shareable + workflow surface coherent.
Annotations persist as FindingType=analyst.annotation rows on AnalystReview/ReviewSession (not a new entity) — uses existing entity shapes.

History tab filters audit.events by EntityType=InspectionCase + EntityId=caseId only (top 200). Verdict/submission/scan-row events whose EntityIds are non-case live in their own surfaces — flagged for B-followup.

items.title: EdgeReplayEndpoint.HandleAsync refactored from single-hint (audit.event.replay only) to per-EventTypeHint dispatcher. ProcessOneAsync now: common pre-checks (auth + future-timestamp) -> ResolveAuditMetadata(hint, payload) -> common audit-row write. Three supported hints: audit.event.replay (Sprint 11; payload=DomainEvent shape), inspection.scan.captured (Sprint 17; EventType fixed, EntityType=ScanArtifact, EntityId=sourcePath), inspection.scanner.status.changed (Sprint 17; EventType fixed, EntityType=ScannerDeviceInstance, EntityId=scannerId).

IEdgeEventCapture gained 3 typed helpers — CaptureAuditEventAsync (Sprint 11 path), CaptureScanCapturedAsync, CaptureScannerStatusChangedAsync. New EdgeScanCaptured + EdgeScannerStatusChanged payload records map 1:1 to the server's resolvers. Edge-side argument validation rejects empty scannerId/sourcePath/status before SQLite write. New EdgeEventTypes static class centralises hint constants.

+15 tests: 8 server-side multi-event dispatch (scan-captured happy path + missing-scannerId reject; scanner-status-changed happy path + missing-status reject; mixed-batch with all 3 hints in one /api/edge/replay; unsupported-hint enumerates all 3 supported; ResolveAuditMetadata unit tests for null/failed cases) + 7 edge-side typed helpers (each hint writes the right discriminator; FIFO order preserved across mixed types; argument validation rejects missing fields). Total now 317/317 (was 302). Existing Unsupported_eventTypeHint_rejected_per_entry switched to voucher.disbursed (genuinely unsupported now).

ratifications.title: scan-captured handler does NOT call CaseWorkflowService.IngestRawArtifactAsync and scanner-status-changed handler does NOT update ScannerDeviceInstance.LastReportedStatus — the audit.events row IS the durable record, an inspection-side projector consuming the audit rows lands in a follow-on sprint. EdgeReplayEndpoint takes only AuditDbContext today; cross-DB writes from this entry point would force a second context registration that's better deferred.
EdgeReplayEndpoint.HandleAsync still writes to audit.events only (existing register entry, existing OR app.tenant_id = '-1' opt-in clause). No new system-context callers; no new RLS policies needed.
Unsupported-hint per-entry error now lists all 3 supported hints in the message so an edge node operator running a stale build sees what changed without grepping the server source.

items.title: ExternalSystemBindingScope extended from PerLocation/Shared to PerLocation/Shared/SubsetOfLocations. Existing rows keep their integer values (0/1); new SubsetOfLocations = 2. Storage no-op — the scope column is already integer NOT NULL via HasConversion<int>(). Empty marker migration Add_ExternalSystemSubsetScope (20260504113030) for deployment-history breadcrumb. New ExternalSystemBindingScopeValidation static class enforces per-scope cardinality: PerLocation = 1 binding, SubsetOfLocations >= 2 bindings, Shared = 0 bindings.

New ExternalSystemAdminService in NickERP.Inspection.Application/ExternalSystems/ centralises per-scope validation + Locations FK existence check + atomic binding writes. ResolveServingInstancesAsync(locationId) is the canonical 'which external systems serve this location?' lookup — three-scope dispatch via UNION (Shared instances always match + bound instances match for PerLocation/SubsetOfLocations).

/external-systems Razor page exposes the third Subset option with checkbox multi-select for locations. Page delegates to ExternalSystemAdminService.RegisterAsync so validation errors surface cleanly. Bindings count column added to the existing-instances table.

+16 tests: 4 pure-function validation (PerLocation requires 1, Subset requires >=2, Shared requires 0, negative count rejected) + 7 RegisterAsync paths (each scope happy path; per-scope mismatch rejects; unknown location rejects; duplicate location rejects) + 4 ResolveServingInstancesAsync (PerLocation match-only-its-location, Subset match-its-members, Shared match-every-location, inactive excluded) + 1 mixed. Total now 302/302 (was 286).

ratifications.title: No DDL change required because the existing ExternalSystemBinding junction table already supported many-to-many; the only thing that grew was the discriminator + per-scope rules. Empty migration is intentional — kept so the InspectionDbContextModelSnapshot stays in sync with the post-extension model.

Per-scope cardinality is enforced in the application service, not as a DB CHECK constraint, because the rule is 'count of binding rows' which Postgres can't express as a per-row CHECK without a trigger; the trigger would not buy anything stronger than the application-layer guard.
PostHocOutcomeManualEntryService doc-comment was outdated ('Scope=PerLocation but bound to no specific location' — actually creates Shared); updated to match.

items.title: Cloned v1 NickHR (8 src + 1 test project) + missing strict transitive platform dep NickERP.Platform.Tenancy into v2 v1-clone/. Source-only; bin/obj/*.user/build.log excluded. Build verifies clean — 0 errors, 240 pre-existing MUD0002 warnings from MudBlazor lints. Integration tests fail identically in v1 + clone (NICKFINANCE_TEST_DB env var; not a defect).

v1-clone/nickhr/V2_BANNER.md (clone-day context, ProjectReference resolution, editing flow, post-pilot refactor plan, two-Tenancy-flavours note). NSCIM_PRODUCTION/NickHR/V1_BANNER.md (committed to v1 origin at ff514d7 — same exception NickFinance got 2026-04-30). v1-clone/README.md updated with NickHR + Tenancy rows.

ratifications.title: NickFinance precedent already cloned Core / Identity / Observability / Web.Shared in 2026-04-30; NickHR's strict transitive dep set adds NickERP.Platform.Tenancy which had to be cloned 2026-05-04. The other three already-cloned deps are reused.

Cloned NickHR's csproj files use ..\..\..\platform\<dep>\<dep>.csproj relative paths that resolve correctly inside v1-clone/ as long as nickhr/ and platform/ stay siblings. No csproj rewrites were needed.

history.name: VP6 — AnalysisService N:N storage + admin + claim semantics + tests

items.title: VP6 storage layer — 4 entities (AnalysisService, AnalysisServiceLocation, AnalysisServiceUser, CaseClaim) + 2 Tenant config columns + 3 migrations. 253/253 tests baseline holds (additive scaffolding).

Auto-bootstrap wiring — IAnalysisServiceBootstrap + AnalysisServiceLocationAutoJoinInterceptor + apps/portal Tenants.razor calls EnsureAllLocationsServiceAsync post-create + InspectionFeatureFlag for portal-only deploys.

Admin UI — AnalysisServicesAdmin.razor + AnalysisServiceDetail.razor + AnalysisServiceAdminService (CRUD with un-deletable + multi-membership guards) + apps/portal TenantDetail.razor for VP6 tenant toggles. MainLayout gains 'Analysis services' link.

Claim semantics + visibility — CaseClaimService (acquire/release with optimistic-lock via ux_case_claims_active_per_case; CaseAlreadyClaimedException carries metadata) + CaseVisibilityService (shared mode AnalysisServiceUser join; exclusive mode most-specific-service-wins). Cases.razor rewritten + CaseDetail.razor renders claim badge. E2E MultiLocationFederation fixture extended for VP6 membership; test green.

Tests — +33 unit tests across AnalysisServiceBootstrap (4) + AnalysisServiceLocationAutoJoinInterceptor (4) + AnalysisServiceAdminService (10) + CaseClaimService (8) + CaseVisibilityService (6). Total 286/286 (was 253).

ratifications.title: InspectionDbContext + IAnalysisServiceBootstrap registration in apps/portal is optional, gated on ConnectionStrings:Inspection (mirrors G2 NickFinance pattern); portal-only deploys without inspection DB still work

Cases.razor's old LocationAssignment-based access path is replaced by CaseVisibilityService; LocationAssignment table stays for raw user-to-location admin tooling but is no longer the access source for the cases list (VP6 membership is)

Exclusive routing tie-breakers: smallest scope wins, then oldest CreatedAt, then ServiceId (final deterministic tiebreaker). Computed in C# from candidate cases — pulled with a 4x take overshoot under exclusive mode to compensate for cases that route away to other services

history.name: Edge auth + image-analysis post-hoc adapter + live-deploy migration backlog

items.title: P2-FU-edge-auth — per-edge-node API keys + rotation flow + handler swap (4 commits a3e7c6a/a0c81f1/cef5c5b/046a979). +17 tests (225 total). Audit register entry landed at a5f7344 alongside merge.

Image-analysis §6.11 — post-hoc outcome adapter (5 commits 4bc8b1e/f1f7622/08ee0e9/d9c6ab5/66647ce). Pull worker + 4-phase rollout state machine (DevEvalManualOnly/Shadow/PrimaryPlus5PctAudit/Primary) + manual-entry stub + reconciliation cursor. Pure entity reuse — no new tables, NoOp snapshot migration only. Worker uses SetTenant (not SetSystemContext). Supersession via append-only AuthorityDocument rows. +28 tests (253 total).

Live-deploy migration backlog staging (2 commits 206c8b3/e7dbcc8). 5 SQL scripts + runbook 07 (~750 lines). Structural correction: 3 DBs (nickerp_platform / nickerp_nickfinance / nickerp_inspection) × 5 schemas, not 5 DBs as in original brief. 32 migrations to apply on first run; 5 already applied to inspection (Sprint 12). 3 irreversible migrations called out. **NOT auto-applied** — operator runs psql.

Pt 2 (email service + first-user invite via one-time link) still queued for Sprint 21. Pt 3 (scoped export tooling) Sprint 22+.
